title: cystopteris crispa
intro: Cystopteris crispa, also known as Bladder Fern or Cystopteris, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cystopteris crispa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Cystopteris crispa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in salves and creams for topical applications.
